description: Dynamic bridge between scheduled runs for April 16 session

market_regime.label: BLOCKADE_ESCALATION_DIPLOMACY_EMERGING
market_regime.key_risk: Iran-US blockade still ACTIVE. April 22 deadline = 8 days. Iran outreach (April 14) opened diplomatic channel but NO formal deal. IRGC 'last warning' not retracted.
market_regime.dominant_risk: IRGC autonomous action risk remains. Any Hormuz incident (mine, seizure, missile) overrides diplomatic signal completely. Brent could spike $10-15 on single incident.
market_regime.crude_current_zone: DECLINING_WATCH_ZONE ($95-100 — declining from blockade peak of $102.26)
market_regime.regime_note: April 14 holiday: Iran reached out to Trump wanting negotiations. Brent fell to $97.76 (-1.61% from $99.36). War premium easing but blockade still in effect. April 22 deadline = 8 days. April 15 morning brief ran: forecast FLAT_TO_SIDEWAYS_UP for April 15 session (PENDING scoring by April 15 EOD). April 16 forecast generated: FLAT_TO_SLIGHT_GAP_UP open, SIDEWAYS_TO_BULLISH session. Key watch for April 15-16: IREDA earnings (April 16), formal Iran-US talks announcement, Brent holding below $100.

prompt_for_morning_brief: APRIL 16 MORNING BRIEF: Today is a full trading day. Key events: (1) IREDA earnings release — watch for loan book >₹70,000 Cr as ADD signal. (2) Score April 16 opening bias: Forecast was FLAT_TO_SLIGHT_GAP_UP (+0.2%). (3) Iran-US developments: Any formal talks announcement = major positive catalyst. Any IRGC incident = negative override. (4) Brent was $97.76 at close April 14 — check current level for regime update. (5) Check P021 (TCS target 2650-2700, check Apr 16) and P024 (Nifty target 23200-23700, check Apr 16) for scoring. ALSO score April 15 session direction (morning brief predicated FLAT_TO_SIDEWAYS_UP). Regime: BLOCKADE_ESCALATION_DIPLOMACY_EMERGING.
